title: Radio Button Group
description: Radio Button Group renders a small single-choice set as button-like options on a unified segmented surface with support for orientation, icons, semantic variants, and per-option disabled state.
intro: Use `ui-radio-button-group` when users need to choose exactly one option from a small set and a button-like segmented presentation fits better than classic radios. It works well for view switches, density controls, mode selectors, and compact settings choices.

featureSections.title: Basic single-choice groups
featureSections.description: The most common use case is a small set of mutually exclusive choices such as view mode or delivery speed.

featureSections.title: Filled appearance
featureSections.description: Filled gives the selected option the strongest weight, while the row lets you compare all semantic variants side by side.

featureSections.title: Tint appearance
featureSections.description: Tint keeps a softer filled feel and is useful when the control should read as active without becoming a dominant CTA.

featureSections.title: Outline appearance
featureSections.description: Outline gives the selected option a clearer edge and is often the safest choice for balanced UI density.

featureSections.title: Subtle appearance
featureSections.description: Subtle produces the quietest selected treatment and works well when the surrounding layout already has strong emphasis.

featureSections.title: Transparent appearance
featureSections.description: Transparent is the lightest option and shows how the variants behave when almost all emphasis is reserved for the active state.

featureSections.title: Icons, size, and emphasis
featureSections.description: Icons and stronger visual emphasis are useful when the choices are few, recognizable, and need to stand out as a quick control strip.

featureSections.title: Shapes
featureSections.description: Rounded is the default fit, square can match sharper layouts, and circular only makes sense for very short or icon-led choices.

featureSections.title: Disabled, readonly, and required states
featureSections.description: State handling matters when some options are unavailable or when the selected value should stay visible without allowing changes.

featureSections.title: Settings panel composition
featureSections.description: A realistic radio-button group usually lives inside a compact settings or publishing panel rather than as a standalone control in empty space.

accessibility.title: Radiogroup semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-radio-button-group` renders a `radiogroup` and each option exposes radio semantics through button-based items with `role="radio"` and `aria-checked`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard handling follows the group orientation.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Moves focus into or out of the group. |
| ArrowLeft / ArrowRight | Moves selection in horizontal groups. |
| ArrowUp / ArrowDown | Moves selection in vertical groups. |

accessibility.title: Labels and disabled options
accessibility.body: Use a clear group label because the visible label is also the primary accessible name of the `radiogroup`. Disabled options should remain understandable rather than disappearing from the choice set.

apiSections.title: RadioButtonGroupComponent - ui-radio-button-group
apiSections.lead: Standalone single-choice button group built on top of the shared field system. It combines radio semantics with button presentation for compact mutually exclusive choices.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Choice set rendered by the group.
Direction of the rendered option list.
Corner treatment of the rendered buttons.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits the selected value when the choice changes.
{ id, label, value, icon?, ariaLabel?, disabled? }

paragraphs: Use this component only for small single-choice sets. If the number of options grows or labels become long, a classic radio group, dropdown, or list pattern is usually clearer.
The component now uses one unified segmented container style. `variant` and `appearance` primarily shape the selected option, while the remaining options stay visually subtle for better scanability.
blocks.title: Notes
